title: Prescription Drug Patient Assistance Programs - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Health Care > Prescription Drug Patient Assistance Programs 3 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF NEEDYMEDS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Website and helpline offering information on programs that help people who cannot afford medications and health care costs, including pharmaceutical patient assistance programs, diagnosis-based assistance programs, free/low-cost/sliding scale clinics, drug discount coupons, camps and scholarships offered for a specific diagnosis, and state programs. Also offers information on lower-cost MRIs and CAT scans and an advocate program for people who are struggling with medical bills. NeedyMeds also runs a drug discount card program that can save people up to 80% on prescription drugs, over-the-counter medications, and medical supplies when written on a prescription blank, and pet prescription drugs purchased at a pharmacy. Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: Helpline: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm Email (800) 503-6897 - Visit Website More Details PARTNERSHIP FOR PRESCRIPTION ASSISTANCE PRESCRIPTION MEDICINE ASSISTANCE TOOL Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A website search tool that helps connect patients to public and private assistance programs to help those with financial needs get access to their prescriptions. Website Eligibility: Varies by program. Programs generally focus on serving uninsured or underinsured individuals. Hours: Not applicable (571) 350-8643 Visit Website More Details PATIENT ASSISTANCE PROGRAM CENTER RXASSIST Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers information about medication assistance programs, which are set up by pharmaceutical companies to provide free or discounted prescriptions to uninsured individuals who cannot afford their medications. These programs mainly cover brand-name prescriptions. Some assistance programs also extend help to insured individuals who meet specific hardship criteria or whose medications are not covered by their insurance. Additionally, the website provides a printable prescription discount card for users. Website Eligibility: Varies by program Hours: Not applicable Email Visit Website More Details No Mapping Information Available
